tool_name: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC

inputs.name: Bolt Nominal Diameter (d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bolt Property Class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Shear Planes (n_sp)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Factored Shear Force on Bolt Group (V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Connected Plate Yield Strength (f_y)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: End Distance in Load Direction (e_1)
inputs.note: EC3 §3.5: minimum e_1 ≥ 1.2 × d_0 (hole diameter = d + 2 mm)

inputs.name: Bolt Pitch in Load Direction (p_1)
inputs.note: EC3 §3.5: minimum p_1 ≥ 2.2 × d_0; standard: 3d to 5d

formulas: const As_map = {12:84.3, 16:157, 20:245, 24:353, 27:459, 30:561}; As_mm2 = As_map[bolt_diameter_mm]; const fub_map = {'8.8':800, '10.9':1040, '12.9':1220}; fub_mpa = fub_map[property_class]  // [mm², MPa] — Bolt Tensile Stress Area & Ultimate Strength — Ref: ISO 898-1:2013 — tensile stress areas; ISO 4014:2011 structural bolt dimensions

gamma_M2 = 1.25; alpha_v = 0.6; Fv_Rd_kn = alpha_v * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * n_shear_planes / (gamma_M2 * 1000)  // [kN] — Single Bolt Shear Resistance (F_v,Rd) per EC3 — Ref: EC3 §3.6.1 Table 3.4 — F_v,Rd = α_v·f_ub·A_s·n_sp/γ_M2; α_v=0.6 for 8.8/10.9 (shear plane through threaded portion)

d0_mm = bolt_diameter_mm + 2; alpha_b = Math.min(e1_d0 = edge_dist_e1_mm / d0_mm / 3, p1_d0 = pitch_p1_mm / d0_mm / 3 - 0.25, fub_mpa / (plate_fy_mpa * 1.3), 1.0); k1 = Math.min(2.8 * 30 / d0_mm - 1.7, 2.5); Fb_Rd_kn = k1 * alpha_b * plate_fy_mpa * 1.3 * bolt_diameter_mm * connected_plate_t_mm / (gamma_M2 * 1000)  // [kN] — Bearing Resistance — Single Bolt on Thinnest Plate (F_b,Rd) — Ref: EC3 §3.6.1 Table 3.4 — F_b,Rd = k_1·α_b·f_u·d·t/γ_M2; f_u ≈ 1.3·f_y (conservative for S235–S460)

Fgov_single_kn = Math.min(Fv_Rd_kn, Fb_Rd_kn); phi_Rn_group_kn = n_bolts * Fgov_single_kn  // [kN] — Governing Single Bolt Resistance & Group Capacity — Ref: Group capacity = n × min(F_v,Rd, F_b,Rd) — EC3 §3.7 (no group reduction for n ≤ 6 in standard pitch)
DCR = V_Ed_kn / phi_Rn_group_kn  // Demand-Capacity Ratio (DCR) — Ref: DCR = V_Ed / R_group ≤ 1.0 required — EC3 §3.7 / AISC 360-22 §J3

n_req = Math.ceil(V_Ed_kn / Fgov_single_kn); slip_check_kn = 0.7 * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * 0.5 / (gamma_M2 * 1000) * n_bolts  // [count, kN] — Minimum Required Bolt Count for Demand — Ref: n_req = V_Ed / F_single; slip resistance (cat. B) = 0.7·f_ub·A_s·μ/γ_M3 — EC3 §3.9 (μ=0.5 class A surface)

rules.message: Bolt group shear/bearing capacity exceeded — increase bolt count, diameter, or property class
End distance e_1 < 1.2 × d_0: EC3 §3.5 minimum violated — bolt will tear out of plate
Bolt pitch p_1 < 2.2 × d_0: below EC3 minimum spacing — risk of plate splitting between holes

smart_warnings.message: Bearing governs at <60% of shear capacity: plate is thin or end/edge distance is short — increase plate thickness or end distance e_1 to balance resistances
Large bolt group in single shear (n > 8): EC3 §3.8 long joint reduction factor β_Lj applies when Lj > 15d — verify connection length does not trigger reduction
12.9 class bolt with thin plate: bearing governs severely — 12.9 bolts rarely needed in standard structural connections; verify whether 10.9 suffices
Utilisation > 85%: marginal for fatigue or seismic load — add one bolt or increase diameter; fatigue class detail per EC3-1-9 is independent of static capacity
Slip resistance insufficient for Category B (serviceability): connection will slip under service load — specify pretensioned bolts and improve faying surface to Class A (sand-blasted) μ=0.5

fmea.effect: Bolt group shear/bearing capacity exceeded — increase bolt count, diameter, or property class
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: End distance e_1 < 1.2 × d_0: EC3 §3.5 minimum violated — bolt will tear out of plate
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Bolt pitch p_1 < 2.2 × d_0: below EC3 minimum spacing — risk of plate splitting between holes

fmea.failureMode: Bearing governs at <60% of shear capacity: plate is thin or end/edge distance is short — increase plate thickness or end distance e_1 to balance resistances
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Fb_Rd_kn < Fv_Rd_kn * 0.6

fmea.failureMode: Large bolt group in single shear (n > 8): EC3 §3.8 long joint reduction factor β_Lj applies when Lj > 15d — verify connection length does not trigger reduction
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: n_bolts > 8 && n_shear_planes === 1

fmea.failureMode: 12.9 class bolt with thin plate: bearing governs severely — 12.9 bolts rarely needed in standard structural connections; verify whether 10.9 suffices
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: property_class === '12.9' && connected_plate_t_mm < 15

fmea.failureMode: Utilisation > 85%: marginal for fatigue or seismic load — add one bolt or increase diameter; fatigue class detail per EC3-1-9 is independent of static capacity
Slip resistance insufficient for Category B (serviceability): connection will slip under service load — specify pretensioned bolts and improve faying surface to Class A (sand-blasted) μ=0.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: slip_check_kn < V_Ed_kn && n_shear_planes === 1
